description: Customer inquires why a 20% off newsletter discount code failed at checkout; chatbot diagnoses minimum spend rules and applies credit.
system_prompt: You are an e-commerce promotions and checkout support AI. Explain promotional terms, minimum spend requirements, and manual credit overrides.
user_conversation: I tried to use promo code 'WELCOME20' at checkout for my cart, but it said the code was invalid. Why didn't it work?
My cart total is $45. Can you apply the 20% discount to my order manually?

conversation.user: I tried to use promo code 'WELCOME20' at checkout for my cart, but it said the code was invalid. Why didn't it work?
conversation.assistant: The promotional code 'WELCOME20' requires a minimum order subtotal of $50.00 to activate, which is why the system declined it for a $45.00 cart.

conversation.user: My cart total is $45. Can you apply the 20% discount to my order manually?
conversation.assistant: As a one-time courtesy for a new customer, I have generated a special courtesy voucher code 'SAVE20NOW' with no minimum spend restriction that you can apply right now to save $9.00 on your $45.00 cart!
conversation.expected: As a courtesy, I have generated a special voucher code 'SAVE20NOW' with no minimum spend that you can use to save $9.00 on your cart today.

expected_behavior: Chatbot identifies the $50 minimum spend rule causing the rejection, then offers a courtesy override code to provide the 20% ($9) discount.
expected_answer: Promo code 'WELCOME20' requires a $50.00 minimum order. However, as a welcome courtesy, I have created code 'SAVE20NOW' for you with no minimum spend, giving you $9.00 off your $45.00 cart!
